methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Hexium is a Seed-stage nuclear energy company founded by Charlie Jarrott and Jacob Peterson, having raised $10M+. The valuation analysis draws from a sector comp universe of 11 peers total—3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private entities including Oklo, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, Fuse Energy, and Applied Atomics.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable and forms the sole quantitative anchor. Using total capital raised ($10M) as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $25M. This method is grounded in observed step-up patterns across comparable early-stage deployment.

Peer-median triangulation is not applicable due to insufficient narrow-set peers at matching stage and valuation tier. While four same-sector peers exist, none satisfied the threshold of three or more datapoints within tightly filtered cohorts (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier), making a narrow median unreliable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $21M to $29M, with a median of $25M. Key uncertainties include the stability of stage-progression multiples in nuclear energy specifically—a sector where financing patterns may differ materially from broader venture benchmarks—and the limited public market depth for direct calibration. Additional comparable transaction data or clarity on Hexium's technical and commercial milestones would strengthen conviction in this range.
